title: Semantic Tone & Persona Checks via Inspector Model
goal: Implement semantic evaluation of model output tone and persona adherence using a single LLM inspector call per model response. The inspector model returns structured JSON with tone_match, persona_match, and reason fields.

in_scope: Implement check_tone(text, expected_tone, inspector_client, inspector_deployment) function
Implement check_persona(text, expected_persona, inspector_client, inspector_deployment) function
Craft inspector prompts that request structured JSON responses with tone_match, persona_match, reason
Make exactly one inspector LLM call per model response (combine tone and persona in single call when both enabled)
Parse inspector model JSON response and extract verdict fields
Handle inspector model call failures gracefully (return error status, not exception)
Reuse existing inference SDK path (ChatCompletionsClient) for inspector calls
out_of_scope: Numeric quality scoring or rubric weighting

inputs: Inspector model deployment name and client
PRD Week 2 section 4 — Tone and persona adherence
outputs: src/inspector.py — check_tone(), check_persona(), and run_semantic_inspection() fully implemented
dependencies: 0_mvp/Part 05 — inference SDK path for making LLM calls
checklist: Create run_semantic_inspection(text, expected_tone, expected_persona, inspector_client, inspector_deployment) function
Build inspector prompt that instructs the model to evaluate tone and persona adherence
Inspector prompt must request JSON response with fields: tone_match (bool), persona_match (bool), reason (string)
Include the original model output text in the inspector prompt as context
Include expected tone and/or persona descriptions in the inspector prompt
Use the inference SDK (ChatCompletionsClient) to make the inspector call with the inspector deployment
Parse the inspector model response as JSON
If JSON parsing fails, attempt to extract JSON from the response text (handle markdown code blocks)
Create check_tone() wrapper that calls run_semantic_inspection() and returns tone-specific result dict
Create check_persona() wrapper that calls run_semantic_inspection() and returns persona-specific result dict
On inspector call failure, return result dict with status='error' and detail containing failure reason
Ensure exactly one LLM call when both tone and persona are checked (combine into single inspection)
acceptance_criteria: run_semantic_inspection() makes exactly one LLM call per model response
Inspector prompt clearly instructs the model to return structured JSON with tone_match, persona_match, reason
Inspector call failures produce 'error' status with descriptive detail, not exceptions
API key for inspector model is not exposed in prompts, results, or error messages
Result dict structure is consistent with deterministic checks from Parts 02-03
review_focus: Inspector prompt is clear, concise, and unlikely to produce non-JSON responses
JSON response parsing handles common model output variations (markdown code blocks, extra whitespace)
Error isolation ensures inspector failures don't cascade to other checks
The single-call optimization works correctly when both tone and persona are enabled
No credentials are embedded in inspector prompts
risks_or_notes: Inspector model may return non-JSON responses despite explicit formatting instructions — robust parsing is essential
Inspector model selection should come from the same deployment pool as comparison models
Semantic checks add latency proportional to N candidate models (N inspector calls)
The inspector prompt should avoid leaking the original prompt's sensitive content if applicable
Consider adding a system message to the inspector call for more reliable structured output
definition_of_done: Semantic inspection functions are fully implemented in src/inspector.py
Exactly one LLM call per model response when both tone and persona are enabled
Structured JSON parsing handles common response variations
Failures produce error result dicts without raising exceptions
Functions are testable with mocked inspector client
No credentials appear in inspector prompts or results
